column2: {419 Operation} edge-burn interaction with its own counter mechanism; fails transfer — affects no other card. Nearest was 6.5, but that owns the Edge as a resource, not cards consuming it
column4: You can burn the edge to burn the card if it has no counter. [ANK 20221011-3]

column2: {Abyssal Hunter} restates its own printed burn-trigger condition (ranged aggravated damage at close range); fails transfer — no other card's ruling changes. Nearest 4.4, but that owns damage mechanics generally, not this card's own vulnerability text
column4: Burns if ranged aggravated damage is inflicted on it at close range. [PIB 20110818]

column2: {Admonitions, The} control-retention rule keyed to being the sole controller of a Sabbat vampire; fails transfer — bespoke to this card's own control-rotation mechanism. Nearest 6.3, but that owns control-taking generally, not this specific retention condition
column4: If you are the only Methuselah to control a Sabbat vampire, you keep control of The Admonitions each turn. [LSJ 20031121]

column2: {Ashur Tablets} confirms play is legal despite an empty ash heap; fails non-obviousness (no stated precondition to violate) and transfer (specific to this card's own lack of a precondition). Nearest 1.11
column4: Can be played with no card in the ash heap. [PIB 20130119]

column2: card effects gated on the vampire being Laibon; fails non-obviousness — a clan/type gate is expected card text, not a general rule. Nearest 1.6
column4: The cards effects do not apply if the vampire is not Laibon. [ANK 20221208]

column2: age restriction is this card's own conditional clause tied to controlling a ready Lasombra; fails transfer — no broader age-comparison rule taught. Nearest 5.1
column4: If you control a ready Lasombra, the chosen vampires may not be older than the acting vampire. [ANK 20210529]

column2: restates the basic "no legal target, no play" rule; fails non-obviousness — any fluent judge knows this. Nearest 3.1
column4: Cannot be played if there is no legal target. [ANK 20191228]

column2: cannot affect equipment no longer in play; same "no legal target" rule as R0119. Nearest 3.1
column4: It cannot be burned if the target equipment is not in play. [RTR 20080808]

column2: confirms {Change of Target} carries no age restriction after this card; fails transfer — a fact about Change of Target's own printed text, not a rule that changes other rulings. Nearest 3.5
column4: {Change of Target} can be played after Bear-Baiting (younger, older, or the same age). [LSJ 20040312]

column2: describes this card's own guess-the-vampire mechanic; fails transfer — no other card shares this guessing mechanism. Nearest 1.2 (only the "each of their turns" periodicity is generic)
column4: Each of their turns, the person subjected to Betrayer can name a vampire. If the guess is correct, Betrayer is burned. [RTR 19941109]

column2: negates the base "predator draws on oust" consequence; this is Bitter and Sweet Story's own printed override — fails transfer, no other card is affected. Nearest 6.5, which owns oust consequences generally, not this card's specific exception.
column4: When the controller is ousted, their predator does not draw cards. [ANK 20230527-2]

column2: the stay-or-burn choice is Blade Clot's own counter mechanic — fails transfer; "allies cannot go to torpor" restates a rule any fluent judge already knows — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 5.3 (torpor) and 5.5 (allies).
column4: The controller of a vampire with a clot counter in torpor chooses whether they stay there or burn. Allies cannot go to torpor, so they must burn. [LSJ 20080612-3]

column2: restates this card's own printed target restriction (cannot be played against an ally) — fails transfer, no other card is affected by this reading.
column4: [VAL] Cannot be played against an ally. [PIB 20111105]

column2: non-interaction between two named cards (Blood Trade / Consanguineous Boon); no principle transfers beyond the pair — fails transfer. Nearest 1.1, and only loosely.
column4: Does not prevent {Consanguineous Boon} to be played. [LSJ 20040531-2]

column2: Anarch-faction-specific hand-off mechanic unique to Blood Turnip — fails transfer to other transfer-effect cards.
column4: When entering combat with an opposing Anarch, they get the blood and decide where Blood Turnip goes (the blood does not follow the Blood Turnip). [LSJ 20080704]

column2: restates this card's own printed target requirement (superior Valeren) — fails transfer to other cards' discipline-level requirements.
column4: Can be played on a vampire with superior Valeren [VAL]. [LSJ 20051012]

column2: scope limit of a control-taking effect against Imbued incapacitation, a narrow interaction specific to Imbued mechanics — fails transfer beyond it. Nearest 5.7.
column3: 100239|Book of Going Forth by Night, The.2
column4: Does not apply to Imbued getting incapacitated. [ANK 20220816]

column2: pure two-card interaction (Botched Move / Dark Influences); no general principle and nothing in the taxonomy comes close — fails transfer.
column4: Is not affected by a {Dark Influences} in play. [LSJ 20081120-2]

column2: position-based crypt targeting plus secret counter-tracking, unique to Brainwash's own mechanic — fails transfer to other cards' targeting. Nearest 5.1.
column4: You can only target a vampire by position, the target Methuselah can shuffle their crypt. If you have no clue (tokens, cards) about a previously revealed vampire's position, you have to take a bet. In case multiple copies of the same vampire are subject to different effects, the controller must track them, but they can do it secretly (eg. {Banishment} was used on one that would benefit from {The Rack} if controlled again). [LSJ 20040728]

column2: restates this card's own printed target restriction (ready acting Brujah) — fails transfer.
column4: Can only be played on a ready acting Brujah. [RTR 19980623]

column2: complex Orun/Laibon lock-and-bleed interaction unique to this card — fails transfer.
column4: You may lock Orun cards the vampire has even if they're not Laibon: in this case none burns because of the bleed. [ANK 20221208]

column2: this card's own action requires the Baali clan/discipline to take — a printed gate specific to this card, not a general requirements principle.
column4: The action to put a counter on the card is an action that requires Baali. [LSJ 20050112]

column2: this card's own mass pool-burn rate (per equipment/location/retainer) restates its printed effect — fails transfer to other cards' cost structures.
column4: Each Methuselah burns 1 pool for each equipment, each location and each retainer. [LSJ 20071005-2]

column2: Chain of Command's own crypt-return condition when its temporary vampires can't bleed; fails transfer — no other card shares this specific requirement-then-return mechanic. Nearest 6.2 (temporary control) but that section doesn't own this return condition.
column4: If none of the vampires can bleed (eg. {Aabbt Kindred}), they all go to the bottom of the crypt. [LSJ 20040518-2]

column2: Card-specific clarification of what Chameleon may target (own vampires, to grant Anarch sect); fails transfer — answers only this card's own targeting legality. Nearest 5.1 (vampire trait changes), which doesn't own targeting scope.
column4: Can be used on one of your own vampire to make it Anarch. [LSJ 20080803-2]

column2: Charming Lobby's own consequence of {Delaying Tactics} (card returns to hand, others barred from the action); fails transfer — the "others forbidden" clause is this card's printed text, not a general rule. Nearest 3.5 (canceled/failed action consequences).
column4: If {Delaying Tactics} is used, the chosen political action card (if any) goes back into its owner's hand, and other minions are forbidden to perform Charming Lobby. [ANK 20201029-2] [LSJ 20030426]

column2: Charnas' own trigger condition (does nothing to an empty vampire that unlocks-in-torpor); fails transfer — specific to Charnas' blood-emptiness check. Nearest 5.3 (torpor state).
column4: Does nothing to an empty vampire which unlocks in torpor. [RTR 19941109]

column2: Card-specific note on which counter types Church of the Order of St. Blaise may place on locations; fails transfer — no general principle about counters, just this card's compatibility. Nearest 1.12 (cards on cards / counters on hosts).
column3: 100347|Church of the Order of St. Blaise.1
column4: Can put counters on locations that use "blood" or "pool" counters. [PIB 20110712-1] [LSJ 20040518-2]

column2: Restates that this strike-type effect only functions at close range, per the cited rulebook resolve-strike rule; fails non-obviousness — a judge fluent in the rulebook already knows this. Nearest 4.2 (range).
column4: Only effective at close range. [RBK resolve-strike] [LSJ 20000110]

column2: Card-specific numeric-allocation constraint of Conservative Agitation's point distribution; fails transfer — no other card shares this exact allocation clause. Nearest 3.7.5 (referendum procedure).
column4: Cannot allocate all X points to a single Methuselah, but you can allocate points to yourself. [LSJ 20000622]

column2: Restates that you may allocate more points than a target has pool, mirroring the well-known bleed-for-more-than-pool rule; fails non-obviousness. Nearest 6.5 (pool).
column4: Can allocate more points than a Methuselah has pool. [LSJ 20020819]

column2: Confirms Consignment to Duat may target a vampire with no blood; fails non-obviousness — targeting "a vampire" never implicitly requires blood > 0 absent card text saying so. Nearest 1.6 (requirements).
column4: [ser] Can target a vampire with no blood. [RTR 20010711]

column2: Card-specific clan requirement (must not control a ready Nagaraja) gating Consume the Dead's burn option; fails transfer — specific to this card's own printed condition. Nearest 1.6 (requirements).
column4: The burn option can only be used if you do not control a ready Nagaraja (no matter the presence or absence of a valid target). [ANK 20240610]

column2: Reminder that Coterie Tactics does not override the normal prey/predator/target restrictions; fails non-obviousness — restrictions apply by default absent an overriding card text. Nearest 3.1 (targets).
column4: Does not ignore the normal prey, predator or target restrictions. [ANK 20180623]

column2: Card-specific requirement gating Council of Seraphim's burn option (all Seraph in torpor); fails transfer — specific to this card's own group of vampires. Nearest 5.3 (torpor state).
column4: The burn option cannot be used if all your Seraph are in torpor. [LSJ 20091204]

column2: Restates that an "or" clan requirement (Tremere or Tremere antitribu) is satisfied by either; fails non-obviousness — reading "or" in a requirement clause is the ordinary meaning. Nearest 1.6 (requirements).
column4: Only requires one clan or the other (Tremere or Tremere antitribu) to be played. [ANK 20190203]

column2: Card-specific restriction (unusable if the action that triggered it also ousted you); fails transfer — tied to this card's own printed condition. Nearest 6.5 (ousting).
column4: Cannot be used if the action ousted you. [ANK 20180326]

column2: mechanics of Dam the Heart's River's own cost trigger (blood payment for a maneuver-strike chosen when range is set); fails transfer — no other card conditions a cost this way. Nearest 1.7, which owns cost timing generally but not this card's own trigger.
column4: [QUI] The opponent must pay 1 blood to use a strike that provides a maneuver if he plays it when choosing range. [ANK 20181026]

column2: restates Deep Cover Agent's own burn-option condition ("all your Seraphs in torpor" = "no ready Seraph"); fails transfer — no other card in this corpus shares this exact condition. Nearest 5.7 (Seraph as special class), which doesn't own ability-activation conditions.
column4: The burn option can be used if all your Seraphs are in torpor. [LSJ 20091203]

column2: restates Derange's own targeting restriction (cannot move it to a vampire already of the target clan); fails non-obviousness — follows directly from the card's printed clan-change effect. No section owns single-card targeting restrictions like this; nearest 1.6.
column4: Cannot be moved to a Malkavian nor to a Malkavian antitribu vampire. [LSJ 19970224]

column2: Diabolic Lure's own pact-counter/pool-burn mechanic; fails transfer — the "counters reach capacity" condition is unique to this card's pact system. Nearest 1.7 (costs), which doesn't own pact-specific triggers.
column4: [DAI] The opponent burns pool whether or not the pact counters reach capacity. [LSJ 20100205-2] [ANK 20221007]

column2: restates that Diamond Thunderbolt (a cancel effect) does not also extend a temporary control effect's duration; fails non-obviousness — a cancel card is not an extension effect, so this follows directly from what the card does. Nearest 6.2, which doesn't own this specific non-interaction.
column4: Will not extend temporary change of control effects when they expire. [LSJ 20040518] [LSJ 20040518-2]

column2: restates the bracket-notation convention that a capitalized discipline symbol requires superior level; fails non-obviousness — any judge fluent with discipline-level notation already knows this. Nearest 1.6.
column4: [dem] can be played by a vampire with superior Dementation [DEM]. [PIB 20130311]

column2: specific incompatibility between Echo of Harmonies' retained political action and Charming Lobby; fails transfer — no other card pairing is affected by this restriction. Nearest 3.7.5, which owns referendum procedure generally but not this specific card interaction.
column4: [MEL] The retained political action cannot be played with {Charming Lobby}. [ANK 20201029]

column2: specific numeric interaction between Eldest Command Undeath and Voter Captivation (counter split 2/0); fails transfer — the split amounts are this card pairing's own text. Nearest 3.7.5 (referendum) / 6.5 (pool), neither of which owns this specific interaction.
column4: If you play {Voter Captivation} on the referendum, you can move up to 2 counters to your pool, and 0 to your vampire. [LSJ 20081002]

column2: card's own ability requires being in play/ready; obvious a burned/ash-heaped card can't act — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.5, adds nothing new.
column4: His ability cannot be used from the ash heap, he needs to be ready. [ANK 20180511-1]

column2: 'X or X antitribu' clan requirement; 'or' meaning either is plain-English obvious — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.6.
column4: Only requires one clan or the other (Gangrel or Gangrel antitribu) to be played. [ANK 20190203]

column2: restates the card's own printed target (prey); no general targeting principle beyond this card's text — fails transfer. Nearest 3.1.
column4: Is directed at your prey. [LSJ 20010807]

column2: confirms the chosen card is visible to all; ash heaps are already fully public zones, so this is obvious — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.11.
column4: All other players can see which card is selected from the ash heap. [ANK 20200523]

column2: cannot burn a vampire no longer in play; obvious you cannot affect what isn't there — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.10.
column4: You cannot use the effect to burn the card and the vampire if the vampire is no longer in play to be burned. [LSJ 20070928-1]

column2: auction/bid resolution mechanics specific to Extremis Boon's own card text; taxonomy explicitly names this ruling as a rejected single-card topic mislabelled G -- fails transfer.
column4: The pool of the highest bidder goes to the target Methuselah. The loosers do not lose pool. [LSJ 20031121-1]

column2: minimum-bid requirement specific to Extremis Boon's own card text; same explicit rejection as Extremis Boon.1 -- fails transfer.
column4: The starting bid must be enough to keep the target into play. [ANK 20190827]

column2: blocks a stealth-then-cancel combo unique to this card's own ability chaining into itself — fails transfer, affects no other card. Nearest 3.2.
column4: [CHI] Cannot be used to get stealth then cancel the [aus] card that provided intercept to the blocking minion. [ANK 20180201]

column2: obviously cannot steal a weapon that doesn't exist; restates a precondition already implied by the card's own text — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 4.10.
column4: Cannot be used if there is no weapon to steal. [RTR 19980928]

column2: restates that the acting vampire must have the discipline the card's own text requires to choose from — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.6.
column4: The acting vampire must have a superior discipline to choose from. [ANK 20180731-2]

column2: ordering between Follow the Blood and Perfectionist named by card — fails transfer, depends on this exact pairing rather than a reusable rule. Nearest 2.4.
column4: If played on a vampire who successfully hunts with a {Perfectionist}, {Perfectionist} grants the blood before combat. [LSJ 20110308]

column2: classifies Fractured Armament's own basic-mode ability as not a damaging strike; fails transfer — only concerns this card's own two modes; nearest 4.3 doesn't own per-card strike-type determinations
column4: [pot] Is not a damaging strike. [LSJ 19970225]

column2: mirror of .2 for the superior mode; same reasoning, card-specific strike classification, no transfer beyond this card
column4: [POT] Is a damaging strike. [LSJ 19970225]

column2: Gambit's own VP-transfer-on-withdrawal mechanic; fails transfer — no other card shares this reward structure; nearest 6.5 (pool/VP) doesn't own bespoke card VP effects
column4: If the controller of a Gambit in play withdraws, his or her predator gets a VP from the Gambit (the Methuselah who withdraws still gets a VP for withdrawing, of course). [RTR 20040501]

column2: confirms Gather only moves a vampire from the uncontrolled to the ready region and cannot rescue from torpor; restates its own printed scope, no transfer to other cards' region-movement rules; nearest 6.7
column4: Only usable to move the target vampire from your uncontrolled region to your ready region. It cannot rescue a vampire from torpor. [LSJ 20030520-1]

column2: clarifies Gemini's Mirror's own protection covers only the vampire and his blood, not other cards on him; card-specific scope of "protect"; nearest 1.12 doesn't own protection-scope wording
column4: Does not protect the minion's equipment, retainers, or any other cards on him. Just him and his blood. [LSJ 20031118]

column2: applies Ghoul Retainer's own printed range restriction to its 1 damage; restates the card's text, no general principle beyond this card
column4: The 1 Damage is not inflicted at long range. [ANK 20210929]

column2: card-specific targeting restriction (excludes uncontrolled-region vampires); other cards interact with that zone differently (eg {Gather}), so no consistent general rule to extract; nearest 6.7
column4: Cannot target an uncontrolled vampire. [LSJ 20061109]

column2: confirms this card's crypt-targeting includes the controller's own crypt; reads its own printed text at face value, no established general default-target rule extracted
column4: Can be used on your own crypt. [TOM 19960419]

column2: restricts Goth Band's own counter-moving ability to cards you don't control; card-specific targeting scope, no transfer
column4: Your Goth Band cannot move a counter from a card you control. [RTR 19980707]

column2: confirms Govern the Unaligned carries no "reveal" clause; restates the plain reading of its own text
column4: [DOM] You do not reveal the target vampire. [LSJ 20010108]

column2: confirms compatibility of {Left for Dead} with this vampire; pure combo confirmation, no general principle
column4: {Left for Dead} can be used on the vampire. [LSJ 20100527]

column2: reads the singular in this card's own search clause as meaning one Kiasyd; generalises only to "singular means one," which fails non-obviousness — any judge knows it; no transfer beyond this card's search
column4: Search the crypt for only one Kiasyd, not any number. [ANK 20210304]

column2: confirms no implicit blood-quantity requirement on this card's own targeting; restates plain reading, no transfer
column4: Can use his action on a vampire with no blood. [RTR 20010711]

column2: Groundfighting's own list of what its cancel ability reaches; fails transfer — a different cancel card's scope needs its own rulings from its own text; nearest 1.8 ("what cancellation reaches") but doesn't own this card's specific boundary list
column4: Can cancel cards preventing the use of equipment if the target has a weapon. [LSJ 20050221]

column2: same cluster as .1 — card-specific cancel-scope boundary, no transfer to other cancel cards
column4: Cannot cancel maneuvering, setting the range or strikes that "cannot be dodge". [LSJ 20050221]

column2: same cluster as .1
column4: Cannot cancel a card that prevents additional strikes or removes the ability to strike completely. [LSJ 20050221]

column2: same cluster as .1
column4: Can cancel superior {Thoughts Betrayed} and {Shape Mastery} (when the latter is used to cancel a strike). [LSJ 20050224-2]

column2: same cluster as .1
column4: Cannot cancel a card that destroys or steals an equipment or weapon. [LSJ 20050221]

column2: same cluster as .1
column4: Cannot cancel a card restricting the use of disciplines. [LSJ 20050221]

column2: card-specific playability condition (two ready vampires needed to gain blood); restates its own printed requirement, no transfer
column4: You need two ready vampires to gain blood. [PIB 20130221]
